title: Vitalvet Animal Medical Centre: Compassionate care when it matters most
bodyText:
Sponsored Content

BUSINESS NEWS - Emergencies don’t follow office hours. Illness, injury and sudden changes in a pet’s condition can happen late at night, over weekends or on public holidays - often when most veterinary practices are closed.


> At Vitalvet Animal Medical Centre, we believe pets deserve access to skilled medical care whenever they need it. That is why we proudly offer a 24/7 after-hours and emergency veterinary service for George and surrounding areas.


Vitalvet Animal Medical Centre is equipped with the latest in diagnostic tools, like dental radiography, an on-site laboratory, ultrasonography, endoscopy and digital radiography.

While we always start and remain committed to the basics that truly make a difference in a pet’s life, the technology and equipment makes it possible to handle and treat any situation in which your pet might find themselves.

We are equipped to assess, stabilise, diagnose and treat a wide range of urgent conditions, from trauma and toxin exposure to complications in chronic illnesses.

For the reason that some pets might need close observation, we have a qualified veterinary nurse for overnight care, meaning your pet is taken care of 24/7.

At Vitalvet Animal Medical Centre, we believe pets deserve access to skilled medical care whenever they need it.

This service plays an essential role in supporting pet owners as we prioritise clear communication, regular updates, empathy and transparency.


> Sandra Nortjé left a 5-star Google review, saying the following: “We are incredibly grateful to the emergency vet team who helped us say goodbye to our fur baby. The kindness, compassion and professionalism during such a heartbreaking time meant more to us than we can put into words.”


Lucille Kotze wrote on Georgiete Staan Saam, a popular Facebook page, that her spaniel had suddenly started to struggle to breath on Wednesday 7 January. After contacting a few practices, Vitalvet Animal Medical Centre came to her aid, and even though it was after 23:00, Lucille wrote that she never felt rushed while saying her goodbye to her beloved Jemma.

At Vitalvet Animal Medical Centre, we understand and know that pets are family, and when something goes wrong, waiting until the morning is not always an option.


> Our office number is 044 050 6000 and our emergency number is 072 088 1957.


We are equipped to assess, stabilise, diagnose and treat a wide range of urgent conditions, from trauma and toxin exposure to complications in chronic illnesses.

We have a qualified veterinary nurse for overnight care, meaning your pet is taken care of 24/7.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
